title: Desplatsia trillesiana
taxonomy: Desplatsia trillesiana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Desplatsia and is native to tropical Africa. Within the Sapindaceae family, Desplatsia trillesiana is part of the large and diverse group of plants known as the soapberry family. The plant has been identified and classified based on its unique characteristics and morphology. Further research on the specific classification of Desplatsia trillesiana may provide additional insights into its taxonomic relationships.
uses: Desplatsia trillesiana, also known as the African yellowwood, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are utilized to treat wounds and infections.
